dependencies: Dependence on limited number of resorts and locations for all cash flow
Dependence on subsidiaries' ability to pay dividends and distributions
Reliance on Macau government land concessions with 25-year terms renewable for successive 10-year periods
risks: Disruptions caused by extreme weather patterns including typhoons and heavy rainstorms in Macau
Supply chain disruptions for construction materials and furniture, fixtures, and equipment
Competition for limited labor resources in Macau
Restrictions on ability to employ imported labor in certain job classifications in Macau

regulatory_risks: Extensive state and local regulation and licensing requirements in Nevada, Macau, and Massachusetts
Gaming authorities have significant control over operations and can limit, condition, suspend or revoke licenses
Compliance with anti-money laundering laws and regulations
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and other anti-corruption laws compliance
Macau government may rescind gaming concession if obligations not fulfilled or for public interest reasons
Nevada Gaming Authorities can require licensing of debt or securities holders
Massachusetts Gaming Commission has broad authority to suspend, revoke or condition licenses
Changes in gaming laws or regulations in various jurisdictions
Changes in tax laws or regulations including rates of taxation
Data privacy and cybersecurity regulations including GDPR and state privacy laws
Smoking control legislation in Macau restricting smoking to segregated lounges
Currency exchange controls and restrictions on export of renminbi from PRC
Intellectual property protection and enforcement challenges across jurisdictions
Labor union regulations and collective bargaining requirements
Visa and travel restrictions impacting customer visitation

sanction_risks: Office of Foreign Assets Control sanctions compliance
Potential barriers to travel, trade and immigration from global political trends
International relations and national security policies impacting visitor numbers
Anti-corruption campaigns in China affecting customer spending patterns
Monetary outflow policies and tighter monetary transfer regulations in China
Geopolitical tensions affecting discretionary consumer spending
Company and industry specific restrictions from government agencies
Risks from operations in emerging markets including Macau
